title: dicksonia globulifera
intro: Dicksonia globulifera, also known as the globular tree fern, is a species of fern in the Dicksonia genus that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dicksonia globulifera include relieving coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
